title: High ventilation breathwork practices: An overview of their effects, mechanisms, and considerations for clinical applications.

assets.abstract: High Ventilation Breathwork (HVB) refers to practices employing specific volitional manipulation of breathing, with a long history of use to relieve various forms of psychological distress. This paper seeks to offer a consolidative insight into potential clinical application of HVB as a treatment of psychiatric disorders. We thus review the characteristic phenomenological and neurophysiological effects of these practices to inform their mechanism of therapeutic action, safety profiles and future

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Bridge paper score=10.5: links Breath/Soul concept to mechanistic neuroscience
